description: Fix timing bug where first-run PRDs without taskPrefix collide on global loop.lock instead of using per-prefix lock files. Compute the deterministic prefix before lock acquisition, then refactor duplicate JSON reads and redundant prefix lookups.

criteria: Rust: No warnings from cargo check or cargo clippy
Rust: All tests pass with cargo test
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless required

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works
3. CORRECTNESS - Compiles, type-checks, all tests pass
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Each task delivers working code + tests together
Test boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: generate_prefix is now pub in src/commands/init/mod.rs
Positive: read_branch_name_from_prd exists in status_queries.rs and reads branchName field
Positive: pre_lock_prefix in engine.rs never returns None when PRD file is readable (falls back to generate_prefix)
Unit test: read_branch_name_from_prd returns Some(branch) for JSON with branchName field
Unit test: read_branch_name_from_prd returns None for JSON without branchName field
Unit test: pre_lock_prefix fallback produces same value as generate_prefix with same inputs
Negative: existing behavior unchanged when taskPrefix IS present in JSON — or_else branch must not run
Negative: must NOT use unwrap() — all fallible operations use .ok()? or .and_then()

Known-bad: if the fallback computes a DIFFERENT hash than init's generate_prefix, two loops for the same PRD would use different lock files across restarts. Test must verify the hash matches by calling generate_prefix directly and comparing.
Failure mode: if PRD file is completely unreadable (permissions, missing), pre_lock_prefix falls back to None and uses loop.lock — this is acceptable and preserves backward compatibility
userStories.description:
Fix the timing bug in engine.rs step 4.5 where pre_lock_prefix falls back to None (and thus loop.lock) when the PRD JSON lacks a taskPrefix field.

The fix has three parts:
1. Make generate_prefix() public in src/commands/init/mod.rs (line 135: change fn to pub fn)
2. Add read_branch_name_from_prd() helper in src/loop_engine/status_queries.rs (mirrors existing read_task_prefix_from_prd but reads branchName)
3. Update pre_lock_prefix computation in src/loop_engine/engine.rs (lines 714-722) to fall back to generate_prefix() when taskPrefix is absent

DO:
- Use the exact same prefix derivation as PrefixMode::Auto in init: md5(branchName + ":" + filename)[..8]
- Read branchName from the PRD JSON using lightweight serde_json::Value (same pattern as read_task_prefix_from_prd)
- Chain with .or_else() so explicit taskPrefix is still preferred when present
- Keep validate_prefix() check on the result

DO NOT:
- Parse the full PrdFile struct (too heavy for pre-lock phase)
- Remove or change the loop.lock fallback entirely — it should still exist for truly unreadable PRD files
- Change the generate_prefix algorithm (must stay identical to init's version)
- Add any new dependencies

Edge cases:
- PRD file unreadable: read_branch_name_from_prd returns None, generate_prefix("", filename) still produces valid hash
- branchName absent from JSON: same as above
- taskPrefix already present: or_else branch never runs, existing behavior preserved
- Generated prefix always passes validate_prefix (hex chars only)

Known-bad to guard against:
- If the fallback prefix differs from what init() generates, the lock file name won't match across restarts. The prefix MUST be computed identically: md5(branch:filename)[..8].
userStories.notes:
Key functions to reuse:
- read_task_prefix_from_prd (status_queries.rs:21) — pattern to follow for read_branch_name_from_prd
- generate_prefix (init/mod.rs:135) — make pub, call from engine.rs
- validate_prefix (db/prefix.rs) — already imported in engine.rs

The engine.rs import block will need: use crate::commands::init::generate_prefix; and the new read_branch_name_from_prd from status_queries.
userStories.title: Compute deterministic prefix before lock acquisition

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: read_prd_hints returns both taskPrefix and branchName from a single file read
Positive: read_task_prefix_from_prd still works (delegates to read_prd_hints or remains as thin wrapper)
Positive: engine.rs step 4.5 calls read_prd_hints once instead of two separate functions
Positive: engine.rs step 6.55 (early_task_prefix at line 816) reuses the prefix from step 4.5 instead of re-reading the file
Unit test: read_prd_hints returns both fields populated for complete JSON
Unit test: read_prd_hints returns task_prefix=Some, branch_name=None when branchName absent
Negative: no double file reads for the same PRD in a single run_loop execution
Negative: must NOT break callers of read_task_prefix_from_prd (check status.rs, engine.rs)
userStories.description:
Currently read_task_prefix_from_prd and the new read_branch_name_from_prd each independently read + parse the same JSON file. Consolidate into a single function that parses once and returns both fields.

DO:
- Create read_prd_hints(prd_path) -> PrdHints in status_queries.rs that returns a struct with task_prefix: Option<String> and branch_name: Option<String>
- Keep the lightweight serde_json::Value approach (not full PrdFile deserialization)
- Update all callers: engine.rs step 4.5 (pre_lock_prefix), engine.rs step 6.55 (early_task_prefix), and status.rs if it calls read_task_prefix_from_prd
- Keep read_task_prefix_from_prd as a thin wrapper calling read_prd_hints().task_prefix for backward compatibility with external callers

DO NOT:
- Remove read_task_prefix_from_prd if it has callers outside engine.rs (check first)
- Change return types in ways that break callers
- Parse more fields than needed

Edge cases:
- JSON parse failure: returns PrdHints { task_prefix: None, branch_name: None }
- File unreadable: same as above
userStories.notes:
Callers of read_task_prefix_from_prd to check:
- engine.rs:715 (step 4.5 — pre_lock_prefix)
- engine.rs:816 (step 6.55 — early_task_prefix)
- status.rs or status_queries.rs (dashboard rendering)

The PrdHints struct should be minimal:
struct PrdHints { task_prefix: Option<String>, branch_name: Option<String> }

For step 6.55, the pre_lock_prefix computed at step 4.5 can be reused directly since it's the same value (or the generated fallback, which init will also generate).
userStories.title: Consolidate duplicate PRD JSON reads into single read_prd_hints()

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: error message includes the PRD filename the user tried to run
Positive: error message includes the holder's prefix when available
Positive: hint explains that different PRDs should use different lock files
Negative: no changes to LockGuard, HolderInfo, or lock file format
userStories.description:
When a lock conflict occurs, the error message should include which PRD file the user was trying to run and which PRD holds the lock, making it easier to diagnose multi-PRD conflicts.

DO:
- Include the PRD filename in the error message at engine.rs lines 730-735
- When the lock holder info includes a prefix, suggest that the user check if the other PRD is still running
- Add a hint about per-prefix locking: 'Each PRD uses its own lock file. If both PRDs lack taskPrefix, they collide on the global lock.'

DO NOT:
- Change the lock file format
- Change LockGuard or HolderInfo APIs
- Add interactive prompts
userStories.notes:
The PRD filename is available as run_config.prd_file. The holder info is available in the error message from LockGuard. Just need to format the eprintln! calls better.

Example improved message:
Error: cannot start loop for tasks/message-archival.json — another loop is already running (300546@arcomp2 prefix=fee28acc)
Hint: Each PRD gets its own lock file (loop-{prefix}.lock). If this PRD just had its first run, the prefix was auto-generated and should now allow concurrent execution on retry.
userStories.title: Improve lock conflict error messages with PRD file context

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: All stale comments about single-loop-only updated to reflect per-prefix concurrency
Positive: Any redundant operations identified and eliminated
Positive: All changes have passing tests
Positive: Findings documented in progress.txt even if no code changes needed
Negative: No changes to public APIs
Negative: No changes outside lock/engine/prefix scope
userStories.description:
Deep review of the locking, engine startup, and concurrent loop code paths to find additional refactoring opportunities beyond REFACTOR-001 and REFACTOR-002. Look broadly at lock.rs, engine.rs steps 4-8, status_queries.rs, and prefix.rs.

DO:
- Read through engine.rs run_loop() steps 4.5 through 8.5 for dead code, redundant operations, unclear naming
- Read lock.rs for opportunities to simplify lock lifecycle, improve Drop impl, or reduce code duplication
- Read prefix.rs for any prefix-related logic that could be consolidated
- Check if the comment at step 6.6 ('Safe because we hold the exclusive loop lock — no other loop can be running') is still accurate now that concurrent per-prefix loops ARE possible
- Look for stale comments that reference 'loop.lock' as the only lock file
- Fix any issues found in-place — this is an implement task, not just a discovery task
- Add/update tests for any changes made

DO NOT:
- Refactor outside the lock/engine/prefix scope (stay focused)
- Make purely cosmetic changes (variable renames without functional benefit)
- Change public APIs unless clearly broken
- Spend more than one iteration — make targeted, high-value improvements

Areas to investigate:
1. Step 6.6 recovery comment says 'no other loop can be running' — with per-prefix locks, OTHER loops CAN be running. The recovery is scoped by prefix, but the comment is misleading.
2. Lock enrichment at step 7.1 writes prefix to lock file AFTER init — but pre_lock_prefix was already computed. Could enrich immediately at step 4.5.
3. Are there any places where 'loop.lock' string literal appears that should reference the per-prefix pattern?
4. The error hint at error.rs mentions 'remove the lockfile' — should it mention the per-prefix filename?
userStories.notes:
Files to review:
- src/db/lock.rs (full file — 918 lines)
- src/loop_engine/engine.rs (steps 4.5 through 8.5, ~lines 706-900)
- src/db/prefix.rs (full file — 126 lines)
- src/error.rs (lines 59-174 — lock error hints)
- src/loop_engine/status_queries.rs (full file)

This task uses Opus because it requires broad code comprehension and judgment about what's worth refactoring.
userStories.title: Discover and address additional refactoring opportunities in lock/engine code

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: All new code reachable from production entry points (engine.rs run_loop calls the new fallback)
Positive: cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check all clean
Positive: Existing tests in tests/multi_prd_lifecycle.rs and src/db/lock.rs still pass
Positive: generate_prefix visibility is pub (not pub(crate)) so loop_engine can call it
Positive: PRD JSON file is read at most once per run_loop execution
Negative: No unwrap() in production code paths
Negative: No dead code warnings for new code
If issues found: FIX-xxx tasks added to JSON with priority 50-98
userStories.description:
Review all implementation for quality, security, and correctness.

DO: Verify the prefix computation in engine.rs matches init/mod.rs exactly. Verify all existing lock tests still pass. Verify read_prd_hints follows the same error-handling pattern as the original functions. Verify no double file reads remain.
DO NOT: Skip checking that generate_prefix is truly pub (not pub(crate)). Don't skip checking that the import paths compile.
userStories.notes: Clean review — all acceptance criteria verified, no issues found.
userStories.title: Code review + final verification
